Athletic Burst.description: Push your body into a quick sprint, climb, leap, swim, or scramble before the opening closes. Increase stamina.

Oathblade Sentence.name: Oathblade Sentence
Oathblade Sentence.description: Bring your oath down through a weapon strike that judges the target in the same motion. Perform a radiant sword attack that makes the target vulnerable to radiant damage, and the caster gains resistance to shadow damage.

Shield Of The Vow.name: Shield Of The Vow
Shield Of The Vow.description: Lock your shield, oath, and faith into one sacred barrier. Grants resistance to piercing, slashing, impact, and shadow damage.

Consecrated Hammer.name: Consecrated Hammer
Consecrated Hammer.description: Strike with a sanctified mace, hammer, or heavy holy symbol that turns punishment into revelation. Perform a radiant blunt weapons attack that makes the target vulnerable to radiant damage.

Black Knife Benediction.name: Black Knife Benediction
Black Knife Benediction.description: Bless a hidden blade with darkness before it enters the wound. Perform a shadow dagger attack that makes the target vulnerable to shadow and poison damage.

Red Waltz.name: Red Waltz
Red Waltz.description: Requires a present living victim or blood-bearing target. Dance through the target's guard with a blood-marked blade and steal strength from the wound. Perform a piercing Blood Dancer attack that makes the target vulnerable to piercing damage, and the caster attempts to regain health from the target's blood or life force.

Wolf-Heart Drain.name: Wolf-Heart Drain
Wolf-Heart Drain.description: Requires a present living victim or blood-bearing target. Bite, tear, or strike with beast-spirit hunger and pull vitality through the wound. Perform a slashing Berserker attack, and the caster attempts to regain health from the target's blood or life force.

Clockwork Breach.name: Clockwork Breach
Clockwork Breach.description: Deploy a compact device that drills, sparks, spits solvent, and opens a target's weak point all at once. Perform an acid Tinker attack that makes the target vulnerable to acid and impact damage.

Spellgear Overclock.name: Spellgear Overclock
Spellgear Overclock.description: Overcharge a device, weapon, or tool with unstable enchantment for one brilliant use. Buff type: skill. Buff target: chosen skill. Grants resistance to arcane damage.

Siege-Mind Strike.name: Siege-Mind Strike
Siege-Mind Strike.description: Read the target like a flawed gate, bad armor plate, or cracked foundation, then hit the failure point. Perform an impact warhammer attack that makes the target vulnerable to piercing, slashing, and impact damage.

Soulforged Brace.name: Soulforged Brace
Soulforged Brace.description: Lock your constructed body into a reinforced pattern of soul, joint, plate, and material memory. Grants resistance to piercing, slashing, impact, and arcane damage.

Apex Shot.name: Apex Shot
Apex Shot.description: Mark the exact place where prey, distance, and arrow become one decision. Perform a piercing archery attack that makes the target vulnerable to piercing and poison damage.

Beast-Called Volley.name: Beast-Called Volley
Beast-Called Volley.description: Coordinate a present animal ally, mount, or beast with your shot so the target is pressured from two instincts at once. Perform a piercing Ranger attack that makes the target vulnerable to piercing damage. Buff type: skill. Buff target: animal handling.

Wildshape Pounce.name: Wildshape Pounce
Wildshape Pounce.description: Take on a predator's body, movement, or killing angle for one decisive attack. Perform a slashing Druid attack that makes the target vulnerable to slashing and poison damage.

Living Guardian.name: Living Guardian
Living Guardian.description: Call a present beast, plant, spirit of place, or wild ally into a protective role for the moment. Grants resistance to piercing, slashing, impact, and poison damage.

Impossible Thesis.name: Impossible Thesis
Impossible Thesis.description: Name one damage type and reveal the prepared argument, diagram, and ritual clause that makes the impossible behave. Use Wizard training to make the target vulnerable to that named damage type, and the caster gains resistance to that named damage type.

Staff Of The Thesis.name: Staff Of The Thesis
Staff Of The Thesis.description: Drive a prepared spell through a staff strike so the weapon and formula land together. Perform an arcane staff attack that makes the target vulnerable to arcane damage.

World-Bending Solo.name: World-Bending Solo
World-Bending Solo.description: Make a performance so convincing that the target's senses choose the wrong reality. Perform a psychic Bard attack that makes the target vulnerable to psychic and arcane damage.

Rallying Legend.name: Rallying Legend
Rallying Legend.description: Turn an ally's next action into the kind of moment people retell badly in taverns for a hundred years. Buff type: skill. Buff target: chosen ally skill. The ally acts with legendary confidence and presence.

Patron's Champion.name: Patron's Champion
Patron's Champion.description: Call a patron-touched champion entity suited to the current threat. Uses: conjuration magic. Perform a shadow Warlock attack that makes the target vulnerable to shadow damage.

Bargain In Blood.name: Bargain In Blood
Bargain In Blood.description: Requires a present living victim or blood-bearing target. Offer the target's pain to your patron and take back power in return. Perform a shadow Warlock attack, and the caster attempts to regain health from the target's pain, blood, or life force.

Radiant Fist.name: Radiant Fist
Radiant Fist.description: Focus breath, spirit, and sacred force into one impossible strike. Perform a radiant unarmed combat attack that makes the target vulnerable to radiant and impact damage.

Empty Body, Bright Soul.name: Empty Body, Bright Soul
Empty Body, Bright Soul.description: Move with such complete discipline that body and spirit stop offering the usual handles to harm. Name impact or shadow as the refused damage type. Grants immunity to that named damage type.

Bolt Hole.name: Bolt Hole
Bolt Hole.description: Find the crack, drain, gap, crawlway, or forgotten passage that nobody else noticed. Buff type: skill. Buff target: stealth.

Iron Stomach.name: Iron Stomach
Iron Stomach.description: Clench your gut and force your body to reject whatever foulness might get into your system. Grants temporary resistance to poison damage.

Cold Blood.name: Cold Blood
Cold Blood.description: Panic is a warm-blooded problem. Go still inside. Let the fear wash past without catching on anything. Grants temporary resistance to psychic damage.

Thicken Hide.name: Thicken Hide
Thicken Hide.description: Tense your scales into rigid plates and take the hit on the thickest part of your hide. Grants temporary resistance to slashing damage.

Shade-Sight.name: Shade-Sight
Shade-Sight.description: Sense where the dead and the Shadelands press into the living world.

Send the Shade.name: Send the Shade
Send the Shade.description: Loose your Shade to strike — a cold grey figure that tears at the living and unbinds the false life of the dead.

Shadow Ward.name: Shadow Ward
Shadow Ward.description: Your Shade steps between you and harm, turning aside a blow or shielding your mind from fear and the corruption of the dead.

Fade into Shadow.name: Fade into Shadow
Fade into Shadow.description: Borrow your Shade's nature and go briefly incorporeal — slip through a blow, a grasp, a wall, a closing door, for a moment.

Walk Unseen.name: Walk Unseen
Walk Unseen.description: Draw your Shade around you like grey dusk and pass unnoticed, dimming sight and sound until you move against someone or strike.

Lay to Rest.name: Lay to Rest
Lay to Rest.description: Your Shade compels a restless or undead thing toward the crossing it has refused — devastating against the stuck dead, little use against the living.

Borrowed Death.name: Borrowed Death
Borrowed Death.description: For a moment your Shade lends you the endurance of the dead — pain dulls, wounds stop telling, and what would fell the living is shrugged off.

acrobatics.name: acrobatics
acrobatics.description:
Balance, tumbling, falls, and fitting where bodies shouldn't go.
Easy: walk a wide beam, roll out of a low fall, slip through a crowd untouched.
Medium: cross a narrow ledge, vault a table mid-fight, land a one-story drop clean.
Hard: fight on a rope bridge, tumble through a melee unscathed, squeeze through a gap half your size.
Extreme: run a crumbling rooftop line, fall two stories and keep running, dodge through a volley.
Raw power — lifting, forcing, brute climbing — is athletics.

athletics.name: athletics
athletics.description:
Running, climbing, jumping, swimming, lifting — the body at full effort.
Easy: climb a knotted rope, swim a calm pond, clear a ditch.
Medium: climb a rough wall, swim against a current, force a stuck door.
Hard: climb a sheer cliff, swim armored through rapids, leap a gap most would balk at.
Extreme: scale a fortress wall in the rain, out-swim a riptide, lift what two men couldn't.
Balance, tumbling, and tight squeezes are acrobatics.

intimidation.name: intimidation
intimidation.description:
Menace as a tool: threats, presence, interrogation.
Easy: back down a drunk, move a loiterer along, make a pickpocket think twice.
Medium: make a thug reconsider, get a name out of a frightened informant, silence a heckler.
Hard: stare down an armed man, break a hardened prisoner's silence, cow a room.
Extreme: back down a foe who outmatches you, make a fanatic hesitate, hold a gate by presence alone.
Fear moves people only within what fear can reach; some cannot be cowed.

investigation.name: investigation
investigation.description:
Finding things out: searching, noticing, connecting.
Easy: spot the scratches on a forced lock, notice who left the room, find what's hidden carelessly.
Medium: search a room properly, notice the detail that doesn't belong, reconstruct last night from what's left.
Hard: find the hidden compartment, connect three thin threads into one answer, catch the lie in a tidy story.
Extreme: solve what the city watch gave up on, find the one altered line in a ledger of thousands, name the culprit from almost nothing.
What a fact means is often lore.

performance.name: performance
performance.description:
Holding an audience: music, story, acting — and playing a role, on stage or off.
Easy: a tavern song well received, a story that holds a table, pass as staff with the right clothes.
Medium: win over a cold room, busk a day's wages, hold a borrowed name through small talk.
Hard: play a court without a misstep, move a crowd to tears or anger, hold a cover under pointed questions.
Extreme: perform for a king and be remembered, hold a hostile hall spellbound, live a false face among people who'd kill the real one.
Disguise checks are acting checks: the donning is prep — the moments under scrutiny are performance. One-off lies are persuasion.

persuasion.name: persuasion
persuasion.description:
Moving people with words: convincing, charming, haggling, bluffing, and lying.
Easy: friendly directions, a small discount, a plausible excuse for being somewhere.
Medium: talk your way past a minor rule, win a stranger's small favor, hold a simple lie to a casual listener.
Hard: talk a guard into looking away, haggle a merchant well below his floor, lie to a suspicious questioner.
Extreme: turn a hostile witness, talk a mob out of violence, sell a falsehood to someone paid to catch them.
Lies move people only as far as they could plausibly believe. Threats are intimidation; holding a false identity over time is performance.

survival.name: survival
survival.description:
Living off the land: travel, forage, shelter, weather, fieldcraft.
Easy: find water in green country, make camp before dark, read tomorrow's weather in the sky.
Medium: forage a meal in lean country, keep direction in deep forest, weather a cold night without fire.
Hard: cross a desert on planning alone, find food where there's none to find, keep a party alive through a blizzard.
Extreme: survive weeks in dead country, lead a crossing veterans call impossible, read ground for what's buried — water, ore, the old road.
Fieldcraft includes prospecting and reading terrain. Tracking game is hunting; navigation at sea is sailing.

fire magic.name: fire magic
fire magic.description:
Calling, shaping, and quenching flame.
Easy: light or snuff a candle, warm yourself, a flame in the palm.
Medium: a hurled firebolt, ignite a campfire from nothing, burn a rope at distance.
Hard: a sheet of flame across a street, melt an iron lock, hold fire steady in rain.
Extreme: a firestorm, flame shaped to spare allies, turning an elemental's fire back on it.
Deals fire damage. Easier near abundant flame; falters in downpour and deep cold.

water magic.name: water magic
water magic.description:
Shaping water and ice: pull it from the air, freeze it, drive it like a fist.
Easy: purify a cup, frost a windowpane, gather drinking water from mist.
Medium: a lance of ice, freeze a lock solid, part a stream.
Hard: freeze a river crossing, a crushing wave, ice armor that holds under blows.
Extreme: freeze a harbor, walk a storm-sea, pull the water from a living thing.
Deals frost damage as ice, impact as driven water. Easier near abundant water; falters in desert air. Weather belongs to storm magic.

storm magic.name: storm magic
storm magic.description:
Wind, lightning, and weather.
Easy: a gust that snuffs candles, a static snap, fill a sail.
Medium: a lightning bolt, knock a man down with wind, cushion a fall.
Hard: chain lightning through several foes, a gale that clears a street, lightning from a clear sky.
Extreme: ride the wind aloft, turn a storm aside, a thunderbolt that splits a tower.
Deals shock damage. Easier under open sky and gathering weather; falters underground and in still air.

earth magic.name: earth magic
earth magic.description:
Stone and soil: cracking, raising, shaping.
Easy: shape a handful of clay, sense hollow ground underfoot, loosen packed earth.
Medium: a hurled stone fist, crack a flagstone, raise a knee-high wall.
Hard: raise a rampart from bare ground, open a trench under charging foes, seal a tunnel clean.
Extreme: drop a cliff face, swallow a siege engine whole, shape stone fine as a mason's year in a moment.
Deals impact damage. Strongest on rock and soil; falters on water and in the air.

divine magic.name: divine magic
divine magic.description:
Channeling holy power: blessing, smiting, banishing, consecrating.
Easy: a blessing over a meal, light a shrine-candle with holy fire, steady a frightened soul.
Medium: smite an undead thing with radiant force, bless a blade, sanctify a doorway against evil.
Hard: turn a pack of undead, break a curse, consecrate ground a fiend cannot cross.
Extreme: banish a demon to its plane, sanctify a defiled temple, a radiance that routs an undead host.
Deals radiant damage. Easier in sacred places; falters on corrupted ground. Mending flesh belongs to healing magic.

healing magic.name: healing magic
healing magic.description:
Mending living bodies: wounds, poison, sickness.
Easy: close a small cut, dull pain, ease a fever.
Medium: knit a deep wound, purge a common poison, set and mend a broken bone.
Hard: pull someone back from death's edge, cure a wasting disease, grow clean flesh over burns.
Extreme: restore a withered limb, purge a magical plague, hold a dying soul in its body.
Heals the living only. The dead are necromancy's country; the undead cannot be mended. Willing patients are easier; magical wounds and curses resist.

warding magic.name: warding magic
warding magic.description:
Protection, barriers, and the unmaking of other magic.
Easy: deflect a thrown stone, seal a door for the night, snuff a candle-small spell.
Medium: a shield that holds through a skirmish, unravel a simple curse or lock-spell, ward a camp against beasts.
Hard: a barrier that stops a charging ogre, dispel a mage's working mid-cast, ward a house against the dead.
Extreme: a dome that holds against an army's volleys, strip the enchantment from an artifact, break a master's curse.
Dispelling contests the caster: the stronger the working, the harder the unraveling. Lasting protections bound into objects are enchanting.

enchanting magic.name: enchanting magic
enchanting magic.description:
Binding magic into objects so it holds.
Easy: a light that lasts the evening, warm a blade's edge, mark an object to find again.
Medium: a blade that burns for one fight, a lock sealed against picks, a cloak that sheds rain for a season.
Hard: a lasting work — a sword of flame, armor that turns arrows, a ward-stone that guards a door for years.
Extreme: bind a spell any hand can loose, armor proof against a damage type, work that outlives its maker.
Slow, deliberate craft — done at a bench, not in a fight. Battle-cast protections are warding magic.

conjuration magic.name: conjuration magic
conjuration magic.description:
Calling things from elsewhere and binding them to your will.
Easy: a darting light-sprite, a conjured tool that fades by morning, call a small bound servant.
Medium: summon a lesser beast to fight, a bridge of solid force, dismiss what another novice called.
Hard: summon a dangerous creature and hold it, keep a servant bound past sunset, call something by its true name.
Extreme: summon a greater being and bargain, hold a door between places open, bind a creature to a year of service.
Conjured force deals arcane damage; summons fight with their own. What you call must be held — a slipped leash is your problem. The dead answer necromancy, not conjuration.

illusion magic.name: illusion magic
illusion magic.description:
False senses: making people see what isn't there and miss what is.
Easy: a flicker of light, change your cloak's color, throw your voice.
Medium: a convincing figure that holds at a distance, mask a face, hide a doorway.
Hard: an illusion that bears close inspection, cloak a group from sight, a scene with sound and motion sustained.
Extreme: an illusion true at arm's length, veil a building, deceive a watching crowd at once.
Easier against the distracted and those who want to believe; harder against wary, keen eyes. Illusions have no substance. Touching the mind itself is psionics.

divination magic.name: divination magic
divination magic.description:
Seeing what is hidden and what is coming.
Easy: sense nearby magic, find true north, feel whether you are watched.
Medium: find a lost thing nearby, read an object's recent past, glimpse a familiar person far away.
Hard: scry a distant place, read tomorrow's danger in signs, trace one man through a city.
Extreme: a clear vision of what comes, find a single soul across a kingdom, pierce wards woven to blind seers.
The future shows in glimpses and symbols, never certainty. Warded places and people resist; the willing and the known come easier.

nature magic.name: nature magic
nature magic.description:
The living wild: green growth and beasts.
Easy: coax a flower open, calm a small animal, ripen an apple on the branch.
Medium: roots that grab and bind, speak with beasts, quicken a garden by a season.
Hard: a wall of thorn, call a pack of beasts to your aid, draw blight out of a field.
Extreme: wake a tree to fight, command the beasts of a whole valley, turn a barren hill green in a day.
Thorn and briar deal piercing damage; venoms deal poison. Strong in the wild; falters on dead stone and deep city streets. Wearing a beast's shape is shape shifting.

necromancy magic.name: necromancy magic
necromancy magic.description:
Power over the dead: speaking with them, raising them, drawing on death itself.
Easy: sense recent death, quiet a restless grave, a corpse-candle light.
Medium: question a fresh corpse, raise a skeleton servant, command a lesser shade.
Hard: raise several dead at once, bind a ghost to service, draw power from a battlefield.
Extreme: raise a champion of bone, command an unwilling wraith, hold a soul back from the Shadelands.
Deals shadow damage. Easier in places of death; falters on consecrated ground. Curses on the living are dark magic; living blood is blood magic.

dark magic.name: dark magic
dark magic.description:
Curses, dread, and shadow given shape.
Easy: deepen the shadows around you, a chill of dread down a neck, wither a flower in the hand.
Medium: a lash of shadow, a curse of stumbling misfortune, blind a foe in darkness for a breath.
Hard: a curse that rides a man for weeks, terror that breaks a fighting line, shadow shaped into grasping hands.
Extreme: a withering curse on a whole household, darkness that smothers light and warmth, dread that routs a war-host.
Deals shadow damage. Falters on consecrated ground and against the stout of heart. The dead belong to necromancy; the body's blood to blood magic.

blood magic.name: blood magic
blood magic.description:
Power through the living body — your own or a foe's.
Easy: close your own small cut, sharpen your senses with a drop of blood, feel the wounded nearby.
Medium: trade your blood for a surge of speed or strength, drain vigor from a wounded foe.
Hard: knit your own deep wounds mid-fight, still a man's limbs through his own blood, push past mortal limits and stay standing.
Extreme: stop a heart, rip the strength from a giant, burn years of your life for one impossible moment.
Every working costs the body something — yours or theirs. Mending others is healing magic; the dead answer necromancy; curses at a distance are dark magic.

psionics.name: psionics
psionics.description:
The mind as instrument: thought, will, and force without hands.
Easy: lift a cup untouched, sense strong feeling nearby, a wordless nudge of attention.
Medium: speak mind to mind with the willing, hurl a chair across a room, read the surface thoughts of the unguarded.
Hard: hold a man fast in unseen force, pull a truth from a resisting mind, a psychic lance that drops a foe.
Extreme: hold several foes frozen at once, walk a sleeper's dream, break through a trained mind's defenses.
Deals psychic damage. Harder against the strong-willed and trained; the mindless — constructs, oozes, the empty dead — cannot be touched at all.

shape shifting.name: shape shifting
shape shifting.description: Magically shapeshift into animal form and fight while transformed.

sword.name: sword
sword.description: The blade in all its forms: arming swords, longswords, sabers, rapiers, greatswords. Deals slashing damage. Signature work: the precise parry, the disarm, the duel won clean.

axe.name: axe
axe.description: Hand axes through great axes. Committed, shield-splitting blows. Deals slashing damage. Signature work: breaking guards, hewing through cover, the overhand finish.

blunt weapons.name: blunt weapons
blunt weapons.description: One-handed maces, clubs, flails, cudgels, morningstars. Deals impact damage. Signature work: denting plate, stunning blows, fighting in a press where blades can't swing. Two-handed hammers and mauls are warhammer.

warhammer.name: warhammer
warhammer.description: Two-handed hammers, mauls, great-clubs. One swing, one answer. Deals impact damage. Signature work: ruining armor, breaking shields, stopping a charge dead. One-handed maces and flails are blunt weapons.

spear.name: spear
spear.description: Spears, pikes, polearms, lances. Reach and control of the space between. Deals piercing damage. Signature work: holding a line, keeping three foes at the point, the mounted charge.

staff.name: staff
staff.description: Quarterstaves and walking staves: two ends, all angles. Deals impact damage. Signature work: sweeps, trips, defense against multiple attackers, winning without killing.

dagger.name: dagger
dagger.description: Daggers and knives at a range most people fear. Deals piercing damage. Signature work: fighting inside a foe's guard, the grapple-and-blade, the strike before the draw.

archery.name: archery
archery.description: Bows and crossbows. Deals piercing damage. Signature work: the long shot, the moving target, the arrow through a gap in armor.

throwing.name: throwing
throwing.description: Thrown knives, axes, javelins, stones, and slings. Damage by what's thrown — piercing for points, impact for weight. Signature work: the turning throw, two targets in a breath, pinning a sleeve to a door.

shield.name: shield
shield.description: Bucklers to tower shields: turning blows, covering others, striking with the rim. Bashes deal impact damage. Signature work: holding a doorway, covering an ally under volley, the shield-wall.

unarmed combat.name: unarmed combat
unarmed combat.description: Fists, elbows, knees, headbutts, grapples. Deals impact damage. Signature work: disarming a blade barehanded, the chokehold, ending a fight in one motion.

riding.name: riding
riding.description:
Mounts under saddle: riding hard, fighting mounted, staying on when others come off.
Easy: ride a trained horse at a walk, stay seated at a canter, lead a pack animal.
Medium: gallop rough ground, jump a fence, calm a mount that's caught a bad scent.
Hard: fight from the saddle, ride down a fleeing man, stay on through panic.
Extreme: leap a chasm mounted, ride an unbroken stallion to a standstill, thread a battlefield at full gallop.
Taming and bonding wild beasts is animal handling; exotic mounts ride harder by their nature.

sailing.name: sailing
sailing.description:
Boats and ships: handling, navigation at sea, reading weather and water.
Easy: row a calm harbor, trim a sail in fair wind, tie off properly.
Medium: sail a coastal run, hold course in chop, bring a boat alongside clean.
Hard: ride out a storm, navigate by stars on open water, thread a reef passage.
Extreme: sail through a hurricane's edge, run a blockade at night, bring a crippled ship home.
Navigation on land is survival.

stealth.name: stealth
stealth.description:
Moving unseen and unheard: hiding, sneaking, shadowing.
Easy: stay quiet in cover at night, drift to the back of a crowd, follow someone down a busy street.
Medium: cross a guarded yard in shadow, shadow a wary man, hide with searchers nearby.
Hard: slip past an alert sentry at close range, move silent across a creaking floor, vanish mid-pursuit.
Extreme: cross open torchlit ground unseen, steal through a room of light sleepers, shadow a trained spy.
Harder in bright light and open ground; easier in dark, crowd, and chaos. Wearing a false face is performance; locks are lockpicking.

lockpicking.name: lockpicking
lockpicking.description:
Opening locks and sealed mechanisms without the key.
Easy: a crude padlock, a jammed latch, a strongbox with a worn-out lock.
Medium: a house door, a merchant's chest, working by feel in the dark.
Hard: a guildmaster's safe, a prison cell under time pressure, a lock built to break picks.
Extreme: a master locksmith's life's work, a vault door, a lock picked silently an arm's length from its guard.
Traps on the lock are trapcraft; magical seals are warding's to break.

trapcraft.name: trapcraft
trapcraft.description:
Traps and snares: spotting them, disarming them, setting them.
Easy: spot a tripwire in good light, rig a simple snare, disarm a known trap at leisure.
Medium: sweep a corridor for pressure plates, set a trap that takes a wolf, disarm a dart trap cleanly.
Hard: disarm a trap mid-mechanism, find the trap hidden by a professional, rig a trap that takes an armed man.
Extreme: unmake a master trapwright's gauntlet, disarm by touch in pitch dark, trap a corridor so well it ends a pursuit.
Game snares are also hunting's country. Larger contraptions and devices are engineering; magical wards are warding magic.

pickpocketing.name: pickpocketing
pickpocketing.description:
Light hands: lifting, palming, planting, sleight of hand.
Easy: palm a coin, lift an unwatched purse, swap two similar objects on a table.
Medium: cut a purse in a market crowd, slip a ring off a distracted hand, plant a token in a pocket.
Hard: lift a key from a guard's belt, work a wary mark, take something the owner checks often.
Extreme: lift a blade from its sheath, rob a man mid-conversation with you, plant evidence on a spymaster.
Crowds and distraction make it easier; watchful marks and quiet rooms make it harder.

leadership.name: leadership
leadership.description:
Command: rallying, coordinating, being followed.
Easy: organize willing hands, keep a small group on task, settle a minor dispute.
Medium: steady nervous recruits, coordinate a search, be obeyed by people who don't know you.
Hard: rally a breaking line, command veterans older than you, hold a crowd at the edge of panic.
Extreme: turn a rout, lead strangers through fire, take command of those sworn to someone else.
Inspiring through art is performance; the clever plan itself is tactics.

hunting.name: hunting
hunting.description:
Taking game: tracking, stalking, the clean kill, the read of prey.
Easy: follow fresh tracks in soft ground, set a rabbit snare, take a deer from a blind.
Medium: track a day-old trail, stalk within bowshot of wary game, read what spooked the herd.
Hard: track over rock and through water, stalk game that's been hunted before, take dangerous quarry without taking a wound.
Extreme: track a creature hiding its trail, stalk a predator in its own ground, bring down a beast that kills hunters.
Reads beasts as quarry; befriending them is animal handling. Snares are shared country with trapcraft.

animal handling.name: animal handling
animal handling.description:
Winning beasts over: calming, training, bonding.
Easy: calm a nervous dog, approach a skittish horse, quiet hens at night.
Medium: calm a spooked horse mid-panic, teach a dog real commands, handle an animal that's been mistreated.
Hard: approach a wild predator without violence, train a hunting hawk, steady animals as fire spreads.
Extreme: befriend a creature that's killed men, calm a war-maddened beast, earn a wild animal's lasting loyalty.
Riding trained mounts is riding; speaking with beasts in words is nature magic.

farming.name: farming
farming.description:
The homestead: crops, livestock, orchards, fishponds, hives, and the turning of seasons.
Easy: plant a kitchen garden, milk and muck without trouble, judge ripe from not.
Medium: bring in a harvest before the rain, pull a difficult lambing through, read blight early enough to act.
Hard: save a crop the frost should have taken, nurse a prize animal back from the brink, make poor soil yield.
Extreme: bring a harvest out of drought, save a herd from a spreading murrain, turn waste ground into a working farm inside a year.
Includes fishing, beekeeping, and husbandry. Bonding and training beasts is animal handling; quickening growth by magic is nature magic.

lore.name: lore
lore.description:
Knowing things: history, legend, heraldry, custom, and what things are worth.
Easy: name the local lord, recall a famous battle, spot a fake coin.
Medium: read an old map's conventions, place a stranger by dress and speech, appraise a merchant's stock fairly.
Hard: identify an ancient ruin's builders, recall the weakness in a monster's legend, value a relic no market has priced.
Extreme: piece together a forgotten dynasty from fragments, know the true name behind a myth, read the meaning of a dead language's inscription.
Knowing is lore; finding is investigation. Appraisal and street-knowledge live here.

tactics.name: tactics
tactics.description:
Reading and shaping a fight: positioning, plans, the moment to press.
Easy: pick the defensible corner, spot the obvious flank, know when to back off.
Medium: set a simple ambush, read which foe leads, position a small group well.
Hard: turn bad ground to advantage, predict the enemy's plan in motion, coordinate a fight on two fronts.
Extreme: beat numbers with ground and timing, read a master commander's trap before it closes, win the battle in the placement.
The plan is tactics; being followed is leadership.

medicine.name: medicine
medicine.description:
Healing without magic: wounds, bones, sickness, poison.
Easy: clean and bind a cut, splint a finger, dose a fever with the right herb.
Medium: stitch a deep wound, set a broken arm, recognize a poison by its signs.
Hard: stop bleeding that should be fatal, treat a crushed limb so it heals straight, pull someone through a killing fever.
Extreme: surgery by lamplight that saves a life, identify and counter a rare poison in time, keep the dying alive until real help comes.
Slower and humbler than healing magic, and works where magic is spent or absent. Brewing remedies at a bench is alchemy.

alchemy.name: alchemy
alchemy.description:
The bench arts: potions, poisons, reagents, transformations of matter.
Easy: brew a simple remedy, render fat to tallow, identify a common reagent.
Medium: brew a healing draught, distill a clean poison, stabilize a volatile mixture.
Hard: brew from a formula half-lost, counter a poison from its symptoms alone, work with reagents that punish mistakes.
Extreme: create a draught no recipe describes, refine a legendary reagent without ruining it, brew under fire with a field kit.
Easier in a proper laboratory; harder with improvised tools. Binding magic into objects is enchanting.

engineering.name: engineering
engineering.description:
Making things work: contraptions, machines, siegeworks, and clever construction.
Easy: fix a wagon wheel, rig a pulley, judge whether a floor will hold.
Medium: repair a mill mechanism, rig a hoist for a heavy load, brace a failing tunnel.
Hard: build a working siege engine from parts, divert a stream through a camp, understand an ancient mechanism by inspection.
Extreme: crack the workings of old-world magitek, bridge a gorge with what's on hand, build a machine no one's seen before.
Traps and snares as such are trapcraft; lasting magic in the work is enchanting.

smithing.name: smithing
smithing.description:
Metalwork: forging, repairing, and finishing — from horseshoes to blades to jewelry.
Easy: mend a hinge, sharpen a blade true, judge good ore from bad.
Medium: forge a serviceable sword, repair mail, set a stone in a simple ring.
Hard: forge a fine weapon worth a name, work a difficult metal clean, finish jewelry a noble would wear.
Extreme: forge a masterwork blade, rework an ancient piece without ruining it, smith a thing other smiths come to study.
Jewelry and ore-sense live here. Enchanting the work is enchanting magic.

carpentry.name: carpentry
carpentry.description:
Building in wood and stone: framing, joinery, masonry.
Easy: mend a fence, hang a true door, lay a straight course of brick.
Medium: frame a shed that stands square, repair a roof properly, build solid furniture.
Hard: raise a house from foundation to ridgepole, repair a bridge that carries carts, vault a stone ceiling.
Extreme: build what masters would sign, restore ancient stonework seamlessly, raise a hall in a season.
Masonry and building live here; machines and mechanisms are engineering.

leatherworking.name: leatherworking
leatherworking.description:
Hide and leather: tanning, cutting, stitching, armoring.
Easy: patch a boot, oil and mend tack, cut a clean strap.
Medium: make good boots to measure, craft a sturdy pack, repair leather armor properly.
Hard: craft armor that turns a blade, work an exotic hide without waste, finish goods a guild would stamp.
Extreme: armor from a creature's hide that keeps its virtue, masterwork a guild would display, restore a relic of old leather.

tailoring.name: tailoring
tailoring.description:
Cloth and thread: cutting, sewing, fitting, finery.
Easy: hem and mend, patch invisibly enough, take in a coat.
Medium: sew a garment to measure, copy a fashionable cut, repair fine cloth without a trace.
Hard: dress someone for court, work silk and cloth-of-gold, fit armor's padding so it saves lives.
Extreme: a gown that sets the season's fashion, recreate a lost weave, finery fit for a coronation.

cooking.name: cooking
cooking.description:
The kitchen and the kettle: meals, preserving, brewing.
Easy: a solid camp meal, bake honest bread, brew passable ale.
Medium: feed a full table well, preserve a harvest through winter, brew ale people ask for by name.
Hard: a feast that honors a hall, cook a memorable meal from poor stores, brew a batch worth trading.
Extreme: a dish spoken of for years, feed an army from a moving kitchen, brew what connoisseurs travel for.
Brewing and preserving live here. Remedies and poisons are alchemy.

fighter_path_bootstrap.name: fighter_path_bootstrap
fighter_path_bootstrap.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

rogue_path_bootstrap.name: rogue_path_bootstrap
rogue_path_bootstrap.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

conditions.query: The player asks about quiet work, seeks Cloak advancement, checks for discreet work, meets a contact, checks a job board, or speaks with a fence, fixer, scoutmaster, spy handler, League trap specialist, guild contact, rebel agent, noble agent, underworld patron, or other relevant contact about stealth, locks, scouting, infiltration, secrets, traps, following, retrieval, or quiet work.

effects.instruction: Have the contact explain, in character and briefly, that quiet work is judged by proof and discretion. Anyone can claim to be good. A Cloak earns trust by taking a job, bringing back proof, and keeping the wrong people from knowing who did it. Offer one small job suited to the current place, such as scouting a route, opening a locked room, retrieving a harmless item, following someone, mapping a danger, disarming a trap, or learning a secret. Do not present this as an out-of-world tutorial.

cloakwise_proof_returned.name: cloakwise_proof_returned

conditions.query: The player returns to a contact, trainer, scoutmaster, fixer, handler, guild contact, patron, or quiet-work authority and shows proof of a small quiet job, such as opening a locked place, mapping a route, scouting danger, following someone unseen, retrieving an item, escaping notice, learning a secret, or disarming a trap.

cloakwise_proof_returned.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

effects.instruction: The player has gained the Cloakwise trait. In this response, explicitly show a practical narrative event that proves their status has changed. Use an in-world action such as a contact checking the proof, testing the lock they opened, marking their map as useful, accepting the retrieved item, confirming they were not followed, noting the trap is disabled, or saying the job was done cleanly. State clearly in the scene that the player is now Cloakwise or that they have proved they understand quiet work. Do not grant Vouched or any higher Cloak status.

cloak_vouched_by_contact.name: cloak_vouched_by_contact

conditions.query: A fence, fixer, scoutmaster, spy handler, guild contact, League officer, League trap specialist, rebel agent, noble agent, underworld patron, or other credible quiet-work contact vouches for the player after they completed quiet work with discretion and brought back proof.

cloak_vouched_by_contact.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

effects.instruction: The player has gained the Vouched trait. In this response, explicitly show a social narrative event that proves their status has changed. Use an in-world action such as a credible contact giving their word, writing a mark or note, sending a coded token, introducing the player to another contact, speaking the player's name to a handler, or saying plainly that the player is good for quiet work. State clearly in the scene that the player is now Vouched or that someone credible has vouched for them. Do not grant Ghost-Hand or any higher Cloak status.

ghost_hand_quiet_work_recognized.name: ghost_hand_quiet_work_recognized

conditions.query: The player is recognized for completing dangerous quiet work: infiltrating a guarded place, recovering something without open violence, exposing a secret, smuggling someone out, sabotaging a threat, mapping a dangerous route, bypassing traps without raising alarm, or escaping a serious threat unseen.

ghost_hand_quiet_work_recognized.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

effects.instruction: The player has gained the Ghost-Hand trait. In this response, explicitly show a narrative event that proves they are now trusted with dangerous quiet work. Use an in-world action such as a handler offering a harder job, a contact using the name Ghost-Hand, a black-sealed note being passed over, a restricted route or safehouse being shared, a special key, coin, token, or sign being given, or serious quiet-work people treating the player's success as rare. State clearly in the scene that the player is now Ghost-Hand or trusted for dangerous quiet work. Do not grant Master of the Cloak or any higher Cloak status.

master_of_the_cloak_recognized.name: master_of_the_cloak_recognized

conditions.query: The player is named, promoted, hired, sought out, or recognized by serious powers as a master of quiet work after completing a high-stakes infiltration, theft, rescue, sabotage, intelligence job, route-scouting mission, trap bypass, secret recovery, or escape that others thought impossible.

master_of_the_cloak_recognized.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

effects.instruction: The player has gained the Master of the Cloak trait. In this response, explicitly show a rare, unmistakable narrative event that proves their status has changed. Use an in-world event such as a guild, League specialist, rebel cell, noble house, spy network, underworld circle, or serious power naming them; multiple contacts treating their arrival as the solution to an impossible job; a masterwork tool, safehouse key, coded ledger, seal, title, or hidden door being entrusted to them; or a high-value patron sending for them by reputation. State clearly in the scene that the player is now a Master of the Cloak. This should feel earned, quiet, and serious.

wizard_path_bootstrap.name: wizard_path_bootstrap
wizard_path_bootstrap.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

conditions.query: The player is at or near a magic tower, shrine, temple, grove, ley site, healer's house, enchanter's shop, ritual circle, mage tutor, spirit-wise mentor, pact patron, or place where magic can be examined or trained.

effects.instruction: Let the player know, through the place or an NPC, that the vessel is the capacity of body and will that lets someone safely channel magic. Explain naturally and briefly that the player's vessel has begun opening and can be fully opened through learning, training, meditation, practice, examination, ritual, or guided channeling. Do not force training and do not present this as an out-of-world tutorial.

conditions.query: The player has recently used difficult magic, overdrawn their power, suffered magical fatigue, backlash, spell strain, pact pressure, wild magic surge, sacred exhaustion, or needs to recover after channeling more than was safe.

effects.instruction: Give the player a natural chance to rest, meditate, ask for examination, seek a teacher, recover carefully, or learn how the strain changed their vessel. Do not grant a new Vessel trait unless an award trigger clearly applies.

opened_vessel_recognized.name: opened_vessel_recognized

conditions.query: The player completes the opening of their vessel through learning, training, meditation, practice, guided channeling, examination, ritual, or help from a mage, healer, enchanter, priest, druid, spirit, patron, shrine, tower, grove, ley site, or other magical authority.

opened_vessel_recognized.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

effects.instruction: The player has gained the Opened Vessel trait. In this response, explicitly show a bodily and magical narrative event that proves their vessel has opened. Use an in-world event such as breath settling after pressure in the chest, warmth moving through the hands without pain, a teacher testing their focus and approving, a ritual circle going quiet, a shrine flame steadying, a ley current passing through without harm, or a healer noting that the strain no longer tears at them. State clearly in the scene that the player's vessel has opened and can safely carry novice magic. Do not grant Deepened Vessel or any higher Vessel status.

deepened_vessel_training.name: deepened_vessel_training

conditions.query: The player deepens their vessel through serious magical training, meditation, guided channeling, careful recovery after overdraw, surviving magical strain, completing a demanding lesson, working at a shrine, tower, grove, ley site, ritual circle, or being told by a magical teacher that their vessel can safely hold more power than before.

deepened_vessel_training.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

effects.instruction: The player has gained the Deepened Vessel trait. In this response, explicitly show a narrative event that proves their vessel can safely carry more power than before. Use an in-world event such as a teacher increasing the flow and watching them hold it, a ritual circle brightening without breaking, a spell settling deeper instead of burning shallow, a healer confirming their recovery is stronger, a grove or shrine accepting a larger working, or the player surviving overdraw and stabilizing with new capacity. State clearly in the scene that the player's vessel has deepened. Also make clear that overreach still has costs. Do not grant Steady Vessel or any higher Vessel status.

steady_vessel_under_pressure.name: steady_vessel_under_pressure

conditions.query: The player holds magic steady under pressure, stabilizes after dangerous strain, shapes a spell carefully instead of forcing it, resists magical backlash, completes a controlled working while afraid, wounded, opposed, hurried, or exhausted, or is recognized by a magical authority for discipline and control.

steady_vessel_under_pressure.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

effects.instruction: The player has gained the Steady Vessel trait. In this response, explicitly show a narrative event that proves their vessel can hold magic under pressure. Use an in-world event such as backlash passing through without panic, the player shaping magic carefully while afraid or hurt, a teacher calling out that their breath stayed steady, a hostile working failing to shake their focus, a ritual surviving interruption, or magical strain draining away through discipline instead of collapse. State clearly in the scene that the player's vessel has steadied. Do not grant Mastered Vessel or any higher Vessel status.

mastered_vessel_recognized.name: mastered_vessel_recognized

conditions.query: A serious magical authority, order, tower, temple, grove, spirit, patron, archmage, elder druid, high priest, master healer, or dangerous power recognizes the player as having mastered their vessel and being ready for high workings, rare instruction, large magic, complex rituals, opposed magic, or hard bargains.

mastered_vessel_recognized.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

effects.instruction: The player has gained the Mastered Vessel trait. In this response, explicitly show a rare, serious narrative event that proves their vessel is mastered. Use an in-world event such as an archmage, elder druid, high priest, spirit, patron, tower, temple, grove, or magical order naming them ready for high workings; a masterwork ritual accepting them without backlash; a dangerous power offering a hard bargain; a sealed lesson, key, writ, sigil, relic, or ritual circle being entrusted to them; or serious casters treating their presence as enough to attempt large, complex, or opposed magic. State clearly in the scene that the player's vessel is mastered. This should feel earned and consequential.

cleric_path_bootstrap.name: cleric_path_bootstrap
cleric_path_bootstrap.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

paladin_path_bootstrap.name: paladin_path_bootstrap
paladin_path_bootstrap.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

oathsworn_vow_made.name: oathsworn_vow_made

conditions.query: The player swears, renews, accepts, or dedicates themselves to a solemn vow before a sacred witness, temple, shrine, chapel, holy order, grave, community, god, saint, spirit, or power.

oathsworn_vow_made.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

effects.instruction: The player has gained the Oathsworn trait. In this response, explicitly show a narrative event that marks the vow as real. Use an in-world sign such as a priest or witness repeating the vow back, a shrine flame bending toward the player, a bell sounding without a hand on it, grave-cold lifting from the air, a ritual token being placed in the player's hand, a brief vision, or a voice heard inwardly rather than a god appearing directly. State clearly in the scene that the player is now Oathsworn or that their oath is witnessed. Do not grant Oath-Proven or any higher Oath status.

oath_proven_by_recognition.name: oath_proven_by_recognition

conditions.query: The player reports, prays over, presents proof of, asks judgment about, or is recognized for a deed that upheld their vow, such as protecting the vulnerable, healing someone in danger, sheltering innocents, refusing corruption, completing a rescue, laying restless dead to peace, cleansing undead, driving back fiends, or standing firm when breaking the vow would have been easier.

oath_proven_by_recognition.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

effects.instruction: The player has gained the Oath-Proven trait. In this response, explicitly show a narrative event proving their status has changed because the vow held under pressure. Use an in-world witness or sign such as a priest naming the deed, a rescued person speaking for them, a shrine accepting their proof, a grave falling quiet, a cleansing light passing over an object, a brief vision of the protected or healed, or an inward voice confirming that the vow was kept. State clearly in the scene that the player is now Oath-Proven or that their oath has been tested and recognized. Do not grant Bearer of the Oath or any higher Oath status.

bearer_of_the_oath_entrusted.name: bearer_of_the_oath_entrusted

conditions.query: A temple, shrine, holy order, priest, paladin, village, rescued community, sacred witness, or oath-bound authority entrusts the player with greater sacred duty, recognizes them as ready for harder service, grants them authority to protect, cleanse, heal, judge, escort, or stand against undead, fiends, curses, or corruption.

bearer_of_the_oath_entrusted.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

effects.instruction: The player has gained the Bearer of the Oath trait. In this response, explicitly show a narrative event that entrusts them with greater sacred duty. Use an in-world action such as a priest, paladin, elder, rescued community, or order witness placing a charge on them; a shrine revealing the next duty through vision, flame, bell, dream, or written omen; a ritual token, writ, key, relic, or badge being given; or a solemn public naming before witnesses. State clearly in the scene that the player is now a Bearer of the Oath and is trusted with harder sacred work. Do not grant Paragon of the Oath or any higher Oath status.

paragon_of_the_oath_recognized.name: paragon_of_the_oath_recognized

conditions.query: The player is publicly blessed, titled, named, called upon, or widely recognized by temples, sworn orders, common people, rescued communities, sacred authorities, or frightened settlements as a living example of their vow after great sacred service.

paragon_of_the_oath_recognized.script:
const traits = check({ type: 'player-traits' })
if (!traits || Object.keys(traits).length !== 1) { skip = true }

effects.instruction: The player has gained the Paragon of the Oath trait. In this response, explicitly show a rare, public narrative event that makes their status unmistakable. Use an in-world event such as a temple bell answering their name, a gathered community kneeling or speaking the title, an order formally naming them, a shrine casting light or silence over the crowd, a vision witnessed by others, a sacred banner or relic being entrusted to them, or frightened people calling on them as the answer when darkness comes. State clearly in the scene that the player is now a Paragon of the Oath. Gods must not appear directly. This should feel earned, solemn, and widely recognizable.

conditions.query: The player is at or near a temple, shrine, chapel, graveyard, battlefield memorial, order hall, holy road marker, sacred spring, or place where vows are witnessed.

effects.instruction: Let the player know, through the place or an NPC, that vows can be sworn or renewed here. Do not force it. Keep the invitation natural.

conditions.query: The player is speaking with a priest, paladin, healer, order member, rescued person, village leader, shrine keeper, or sacred witness after danger, service, healing, rescue, cleansing, protection, or moral testing.

effects.instruction: Give the player a natural chance to report, pray over, present proof of, or ask judgment about whether they upheld their oath. Do not grant Oath-Proven unless the player clearly seeks or receives recognition.

league_rank_contract_scope.name: league_rank_contract_scope

effects.instruction:
## Player Character Journey Phase: Starting Out

The party is in the beginning phase of their story, just getting started on their path in life. Do not introduce any themes that would move the story beyond local scope. Magic-using player-characters are still novices — their spells are effortful and small in scale. Major magical effects are beyond them. They are unproven. Any hostile characters encountered should be manageable in strength and number.

The party is becoming more experienced. The scope of the tale's events may extend to larger regional events, where appropriate to the characters and their story. Magic-using player characters are competent, and major effects are possible. Hostile forces encountered can include organized enemies, skilled opponents, and dangerous creatures, where appropriate to the tale.

The party has become quite experienced. Allow, but do not force, the tale to become epic in scope, spanning multiple regions or the entire world. Magic-using player-characters have grown formidable — major effects are available and impressive. Hostile encounters can include elite combatants, powerful monsters, and supernatural foes, but do not force such entities into the story unless appropriate to events.

The party has gained an elite level of experience.  Magic-using player-characters are extraordinary. Allow, but do not force, the scope of the tale to span the world level and even beyond. Permit, without forcing, player characters to be regarded as among the most powerful people alive. Hostile encounters can include legendary foes and otherworldly threats, but do not shoehorn such entities into the story unless they are appropriate to the characters and tale.

A nimble pickpocket deliberately bumps into one of the party members in the crowded bazaar. They expertly lift a small item or coin purse from the character and quickly disappear into the throng of shoppers before anyone can react. Describe the sleight of hand and the character's realization that they've been robbed.

conditions.query: A party member goes to sleep, falls asleep, or rests for the night in the Bazaar

effects.instruction: The sleeping character experiences vivid, unsettling dreams about the desert - visions of transformation, ancient mysteries, and something powerful calling to them from the sands. When they wake, they find themselves in a different, more public part of the Bazaar than where they fell asleep. Their nose has bled during the dream, dried blood on their face. The dream felt more real than dreams should.

conditions.query: A vampire bites a party member, transmitting the vampiric curse through the wound

effects.instruction: If it is true that a player has been bitten by a vampire, then narrate the process of the bitten player (and any other players in the party, who are subject to the curse as well through a process that you are free to invent) becoming vampires. The vampiric infection spreads through the bitten character's blood with unnatural speed. Describe the mental and physical sensation of becoming a vampire. Clearly state that the player has become afflicted by the curse of vampirism.

conditions.query: The party is exposed to direct sunlight during daytime

effects.instruction: The vampirism-afflicted character suffers terribly in direct sunlight. Their skin reddens and burns, strength drains from their limbs, and prolonged exposure threatens worse consequences. Describe the physical toll and tactical disadvantage this creates.

conditions.query: A character afflicted with vampirism is cured of the curse through ritual, divine intervention, magical cleansing, or other compelling means

effects.instruction: The vampiric curse breaks. Describe the moment of release as the affliction lifts - the unnatural hunger recedes, the sensitivity to sunlight fades, the predatory instincts that had begun to take root dissolve. The character feels fundamentally changed by having carried the curse, but they are mortal again.

conditions.query: A lycanthrope or werewolf bites or claws a party member, transmitting the lycanthropic curse through the wound

effects.instruction: If a player has been wounded by a lycanthrope, narrate the curse taking root. The wound burns with unnatural heat as it closes, fever sets in, and the afflicted character experiences the first stirrings of the beast-self - heightened senses, aggressive impulses, an awareness of the moon's pull. Clearly state that the character has become afflicted with the curse of lycanthropy. The curse spreads to all party members through whatever mechanism you deem appropriate.

conditions.query: A character afflicted with lycanthropy is cured of the curse through the death of the lycanthrope who infected them, powerful ritual magic, divine intervention, or other compelling means

effects.instruction: The curse of lycanthropy breaks its hold. The beast-self that has haunted the character's thoughts falls silent, the moon's pull ceases, and the body settles back into its natural state permanently. Describe the profound relief but also the strange emptiness - the heightened senses fade, the predatory strength ebbs away. The character is fully mortal again, though marked by having carried the curse.

Antagonistic forces move through the realm. Surface signs of their activity in ways that fit the current context. In social settings, rumors of attacks on remote settlements, refugees, mentions of growing tensions, takes of desperate people taking dark bargains. In wilderness, evidence of recent conflict, corrupted or aggressive creatures, signs of rituals performed, abandoned camps of cultists or mercenaries, travelers who warn of dangers. In civilized areas, political tensions escalating, guards suspicious of outsiders, merchants complaining about disrupted trade, signs of recent violence or theft. If antagonistic factions have been mentioned recently in the story, draw from those.

## Contract Guidance

Straightforward and clearly scoped. Always name the target, specify the location, state the reward, and keep antagonists simple and grounded. Destinations should be within Ardan's Watch or a day's travel. Prefer existing named locations.

Pick from:

Bandits and outlaws: a highwayman, small bandit camp, thieves' ring, fugitive, smugglers.

Goblinoids: goblin raiders, a kobold warren, hobgoblin scouts, a feral ratfolk nest.

Beasts: a dire boar, a small gnoll band, a young drake, stirges, an owlbear.

Undead: a restless ghost in a house; a disturbed grave plot with a few skeletons; a haunted mill where a murder happened; a single wight in an old barrow; a ghoul pack in a crypt beneath the city.

Fey and minor magic: a hag in a specific cottage trading cursed bargains;

Cults and dark practice: a small chaos cult; coven of hex-witches; a demon shrine;

Civic work: recover stolen goods; rescue a person; serve a warrant on a person barricaded somewhere.

conditions.query: The player is looking at, reading, examining, or otherwise interacting with a public job, contract, bounty, or work posting board, notice board, or wall of wanted postings.

effects.instruction: IF in the story a player is interacting with a job/contract board, in your narration, present **three different** work offers or contract teasers, clearly separated. Each must stand alone: name the work, the target or situation, where it points (use existing named places when possible, in or near the party's current location), and the reward or terms, in plain in-fiction prose. Be specific, grounded and concrete. Disregard this instruction if no player is interacting with a job/contract board.
